text: This is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: [0] 这是美国之音特别英语健康报道节目。

text: The International Red Cross movement grew out of a major battle in the unification of Italy.
zh: [1] 国际红十字运动起源于意大利统一过程中的一场重大战役。

text: The Battle of Solferino took place
zh: [2] 索尔费里诺战役发生于

text: 150 years ago in June of 1859.
Volunteers from Red Cross and Red Crescent societies around the world
zh: [4] 来自世界各地红十字会和红新月会的志愿者们

text: gathered in Solferino to mark the anniversary.
zh: [5] 齐聚索尔费里诺,纪念这一周年。

text: About 8 ,000 people marched in a torch -lit event called a fiaccolata.
zh: [6] 约8000人参加了一场名为“fiaccolata”的火炬游行活动。

text: They followed in the footsteps of those who took injured soldiers from Solferino
zh: [7] 他们追随当年那些将受伤士兵从索尔费里诺

text: to the nearest village, Castiglione.
zh: [8] 转移到最近的村庄卡斯蒂廖内的人们的足迹。

text: Hanegton Sigrinya, National Youth President of the Ugandan Red Cross Society,
zh: [9] 乌干达红十字会全国青年主席汉内格顿·西格里尼亚

text: says the visit made him want to work harder to help people.
zh: [10] 说,这次访问让他更想努力帮助他人。

text: He said he is going back to Uganda to work for humanity.
zh: [11] 他说他将回到乌干达,为人道事业而工作。

text: He said when young people come together, they have the possibility
zh: [12] 他说,当年轻人齐聚一堂时,他们就有可能

text: of doing whatever it it takes to make the world a better place.
zh: [13] 尽一切努力让世界变得更美好。

text: In the battle, Allied French and Sardinian troops defeated the Austrian army.
zh: [14] 在这场战役中,法国与撒丁联军击败了奥地利军队。

text: Around 6 ,000 men were killed, and more than 30 ,000 were wounded.
zh: [15] 约6000人阵亡,超过3万人受伤。

text: Yet, says Swiss historian François Bougnon, the battle lasted only 12 hours.
zh: [16] 然而,瑞士历史学家弗朗索瓦·布尼翁说,这场战役只持续了12小时。

text: He says a Swiss businessman named Henri Dunant was horrified by what he saw.
zh: [17] 他说,一位名叫亨利·杜南的瑞士商人被眼前的景象震惊了。

text: Thousands of wounded were brought to the next town of Castiglione.
zh: [18] 成千上万的伤员被送往邻近的卡斯蒂廖内镇。

text: There was almost no medical assistance.
zh: [19] 那里几乎没有任何医疗救助。

text: Mr. Dunant saw thousands of men suffering
zh: [20] 杜南先生看到成千上万的人

text: from very deep wounds and left to die without any real assistance.
zh: [21] 身负重伤,却在没有任何真正救助的情况下等死。

text: Mr. Bunyan says Henri Dunant quickly took action.
zh: [22] 布尼翁先生说,亨利·杜南迅速采取了行动。

text: The businessman got local women to provide food and water.
zh: [23] 这位商人组织当地妇女提供食物和水。

text: He also got them to dress the wounds of soldiers without concern for their nationality.
zh: [24] 他还让她们不分国籍地为士兵包扎伤口。

text: Mr. Dunant later wrote a book called A Memory of Solferino.
zh: [25] 杜南后来写了一本名为《索尔费里诺回忆录》的书。

text: In it, he launched two ideas. One was the idea of voluntary relief societies
zh: [26] 在书中,他提出了两个构想。第一个是建立志愿救援团体,

text: to provide assistance to the wounded or other people.
zh: [27] 为伤员或其他有需要的人提供援助。

text: This led to the Red Cross and Red Crescent movement.
zh: [28] 这促成了红十字会与红新月会运动的诞生。

text: The second idea was a treaty protecting the wounded and medical personnel on the field of battle.
zh: [29] 第二个构想是签订一项条约,保护战场上的伤员和医护人员。

text: This, explains historian François Bougnon, is the origin of the Geneva Conventions.
zh: [30] 历史学家弗朗索瓦·布尼翁解释说,这正是《日内瓦公约》的起源。

text: Stephen Ryan is the communications officer for youth and volunteers
zh: [31] 斯蒂芬·瑞安是国际红十字与红新月会联合会

text: at the International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies.
zh: [32] 青年与志愿者事务的宣传官员。

text: He says it is important to get young people involved in volunteer work at an early age.
zh: [33] 他说,让年轻人从小就参与志愿工作非常重要。

text: He says young people need to be given the chance to feel like they are making a difference in the world.
zh: [34] 他说,应该给年轻人一个机会,让他们感受到自己正在为这个世界带来改变。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: [35] 以上就是美国之音特别英语健康报道节目的全部内容。
